text.title: ClickUp Agent Integration and Gotchas
text.summary: Agent-driven workspace bootstrapping, recommended subagents, API gotchas, and Python bootstrap script.

rule.statement: Agents bootstrapping a ClickUp workspace must stop and report error on any failed API request rather than continuing — partial hierarchy creates orphaned lists with no parent folder.
rule.rationale: ClickUp hierarchy requires all levels to exist before tasks can be created; a partially created Space/Folder/List structure is not recoverable via API without manual cleanup.

rule.statement: Use Authorization header with token directly (no "Bearer" prefix); implement exponential backoff on 429 with base delay 2s, max 5 retries.
rule.rationale: ClickUp API v2 uses a non-standard authorization format; omitting "Bearer" is the most common integration bug. Rate limit is 100 req/min per token.

rule.statement: Custom fields in task responses are an array under custom_fields; match by field id (UUID), not by name — field names can be renamed by users, IDs cannot.
rule.rationale: If agents match by name, renaming a field breaks all agent queries silently; UUID matching is stable across renames.

rule.statement: Custom field updates require individual POST requests per task per field — there is no bulk custom field update endpoint.
rule.rationale: Bulk task update (PATCH /task/bulk) does not support custom field payloads; each field on each task requires a separate POST /task/{id}/field/{field_id} call.

rule.statement: ClickUp webhooks are workspace-wide — filter by list_id or space_id client-side; follow up with GET /task/{task_id} to fetch full task details after receiving a webhook event.
rule.rationale: Webhooks carry only task_id on most events, not the full task object; agents must fetch the full task in a follow-up request to read field values.

example.code:
import os, requests
BASE = "https://api.clickup.com/api/v2" HEADERS = {"Authorization": os.environ["CLICKUP_TOKEN"], "Content-Type": "application/json"} TEAM_ID = os.environ["CLICKUP_TEAM_ID"]
def create_space(name, statuses): r = requests.post(f"{BASE}/team/{TEAM_ID}/space", headers=HEADERS, json={"name": name, "multiple_assignees": True, "statuses": [{"status": s, "type": "open", "color": "#4169e1"} for s in statuses]}) r.raise_for_status() return r.json()["id"]
def create_list(folder_id, name): r = requests.post(f"{BASE}/folder/{folder_id}/list", headers=HEADERS, json={"name": name}) r.raise_for_status() return r.json()["id"]
example.note: Uses raise_for_status() to stop on error; Authorization header has no "Bearer" prefix per ClickUp API v2 spec.

list.item: clickup-workspace-bootstrapper: given workspace config JSON, creates full Space/Folder/List hierarchy via API and returns created IDs.
clickup-sprint-reporter: queries tasks in current sprint list by status; computes completion %, blocked count, overdue count; posts to Slack.
clickup-intake-agent: webhook listener; on task creation in Backlog list, validates required custom fields, assigns, adds checklist template.
